{"problem_standardized_math":"Show that the quadratic polynomial interpolating the data \\(\\phi(0)\\), \\(\\phi'(0)\\), and \\(\\phi(\\alpha_0)\\) is the one given in \\((3.57)\\). Then use the fact that the sufficient decrease condition \\((3.6a)\\) is not satisfied at \\(\\alpha_0\\) to prove that this quadratic has positive curvature and that its minimizer \\(\\alpha_1\\) satisfies\n\\[\n\\alpha_1<\\frac{\\alpha_0}{2(1-c_1)}.\n\\]\nSince \\(c_1\\) is typically chosen to be quite small in practice, this inequality suggests that \\(\\alpha_1\\) cannot be much larger than \\(\\tfrac12\\) (and may be smaller), thereby indicating the size of the new step length."}
